title: physaria carinata subsp. pulchella
intro: Physaria carinata subsp. pulchella, also known as pulchella bladderpod, is a flowering plant species within the family Brassicaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of this plant include potential applications in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. Physaria carinata subsp. pulchella can be prepared as a tea or infused oil, or used as a tincture, to harness its medicinal properties.
